name: Eggplant Szechuan Style #1
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Oriental eggplant or 1 domestic eggplant (cut into 1/2-inch thick slices, then cut the slices into 2 1/2-inch strips)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine onions, garlic, ginger, and hot bean sauce.
2. In a small bowl, mix chicken broth, soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Add half of the eggplant slices and cook, stirring often, until soft and moist (about 5 minutes). Remove to a colander and drain.
5. Repeat with the remaining eggplant using 2 more tablespoons of oil.
6. Heat the remaining tablespoon of vegetable oil in the wok over medium-high heat.
7. Add the onion-garlic mixture and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
8. Return the cooked eggplant to the wok.
9. Add the chicken broth mixture. Bring to a boil and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid is almost evaporated.
10. Blend water and cornstarch in a small cup; add to the wok.
11. Cook and stir until the sauce boils and thickens slightly.
12. Stir in sesame oil.
13. Garnish with reserved onion slices. Makes 4 to 5 servings.
